chapter_title.en: The Chapter on the Virtues of Prayer

text: 638 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَحَبُّ اَلْأَعْمَالِ إِلَى اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ اَلصَّلاَةُ وَ هِيَ آخِرُ وَصَايَا اَلْأَنْبِيَاءِ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَمَا أَحْسَنَ مِنَ اَلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يَغْتَسِلَ أَوْ يَتَوَضَّأَ فَيُسْبِغَ اَلْوُضُوءَ ثُمَّ يَتَنَحَّى حَيْثُ لاَ يَرَاهُ أَنِيسٌ فَيُشْرِفَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَلَيْهِ وَ هُوَ رَاكِعٌ أَوْ سَاجِدٌ إِنَّ اَلْعَبْدَ إِذَا سَجَدَ فَأَطَالَ اَلسُّجُودَ نَادَى إِبْلِيسُ يَا وَيْلاَهْ أَطَاعُوهُ وَ عَصَيْتُ وَ سَجَدُوا وَ أَبَيْتُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.638 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The most beloved of deeds to Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, is prayer. It is the final counsel of the prophets (as).
How beautiful it is for a person to perform ghusl (ritual bathing) or ablution (wudu'), perfecting it thoroughly, then to go to a place where no one can see him, and to humble himself before Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, in bowing (ruku') or prostration (Sujood). When a servant prostrates and prolongs his prostration, Iblis (Satan) cries out,
'Woe to me! They obeyed Him, while I disobeyed Him, and they prostrated while I refused.'".
